page.title: Hispanic Day Yacht Charter in Marbella: Celebrate Culture at Sea

data.meta_description: Celebrate Hispanic Day with a luxury yacht charter in Marbella. Depart Puerto Banús from €749/2h. Book your cultural sea escape today.
data.summary: Celebrate Hispanic Day (12 October) with a luxury yacht charter in Marbella. Depart from Puerto Banús on boats from €749, with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Hispanic Day (12 October) marks Spanish heritage and national pride.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic marina.
Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39.
Each booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719 for 4 hours, with a free jet ski.
Marbella's October sea temperature averages 21°C – ideal for swimming.
Fleet ranges from 5-pax speedboats to 12-pax luxury yachts.
Spanish IVA (21%) and insurance are included in all prices.

faq.q: What is Hispanic Day and why celebrate it in Marbella?
faq.a: Hispanic Day (Día de la Hispanidad) on 12 October commemorates Spain's cultural heritage and ties with Latin America. Marbella, with its Mediterranean sunshine and luxury lifestyle, offers a refined way to honour the day – a yacht charter from Puerto Banús combines celebration with sea views, fine drinks, and the spirit of Spanish hospitality.

faq.q: Which yachts are available for Hispanic Day charters?
faq.a: Our fleet includes over 15 boats departing from Puerto Banús. For smaller groups, the Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are ideal. Mid-size options like the Astondoa 40 (9 pax, from €749/2h) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) work well for families. Larger groups can book the Mangusta 80 (12 pax, from €4,719/4h) or Ferretti 94 (12 pax). All prices include skipper, drinks, and snacks.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You also get water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There are no hidden fees – just show up at Puerto Banús and enjoy. For Tier B boats (Mangusta 80), a jet ski is included free for the first 4 hours.

faq.q: Can we watch the Hispanic Day parade from the yacht?
faq.a: While the main national parade is in Madrid, Marbella hosts local celebrations including cultural events and tapas festivals. From your yacht, you can enjoy the Mediterranean backdrop, anchor near the coast, and listen to Spanish music on board. Many clients combine a morning charter with an afternoon stroll along the Golden Mile.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for Hispanic Day?
faq.a: Booking is simple: contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on specific boats. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book directly online at the listed prices. We recommend reserving at least 2 weeks in advance for 12 October, as demand is high. All charters depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is the weather like in Marbella on Hispanic Day?
faq.a: October in Marbella is mild and pleasant. Average daytime temperatures range from 20°C to 25°C, with sea temperatures around 21°C – comfortable for swimming. Sunshine is common, though a light jacket is advisable for evening returns. The sea is generally calm, making for smooth cruising along the Costa del Sol.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food or decorations on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food, cakes, or decorations to personalise your celebration. The boat comes with a cooler and basic snacks. For larger groups, we can arrange catering from local Marbella restaurants – just let us know in advance. Note that glass bottles are discouraged for safety.

faq.q: Are there any age restrictions for children on board?
faq.a: Children are welcome on all our yachts. Life jackets are provided for all ages, including infants. The skipper will brief you on safety procedures. For families, the Lagoon 380 (10 pax) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) offer spacious decks. There is no minimum age, but parents are responsible for supervision.

data.body_html:
Hispanic Day (12 October) is one of Spain's most significant national holidays, celebrating the country's cultural heritage and its ties with Latin America. What better way to honour the occasion than with a luxury yacht charter in Marbella? Depart from the glamorous Puerto Banús, soak up the Mediterranean sun, and enjoy the same hospitality that defines Spanish culture. Our fleet offers options from €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40, including a skipper, drinks, and snacks. Whether you're a family, a group of friends, or a couple, a boat rental Marbella experience adds a unique maritime dimension to your celebration.

Why Celebrate Hispanic Day on a Yacht?

Hispanic Day, or Día de la Hispanidad, commemorates the arrival of Columbus in the Americas and the shared language and traditions across the Spanish-speaking world. In Marbella, the day is marked by local events, tapas routes, and a festive atmosphere. A yacht charter elevates the experience: you can toast with cava while cruising past the Golden Mile, anchor at Cala del Faro for a swim, or simply enjoy the sea breeze. The combination of Spanish culture and coastal luxury makes this a memorable way to celebrate.

Our Fleet: From Intimate to Grand

We operate over 15 vessels, all departing from Puerto Banús. For smaller groups, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) offer an intimate setting. Mid-range options include the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – both at €749 for 2 hours. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) starts at €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Other premium yachts like the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) are available on request. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA.

What's Included in Your Charter

Every booking comes with a comprehensive package: a professional skipper, fuel for the day, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Safety equipment and insurance are covered, and the 21% IVA is included in the listed prices. For Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants), a jet ski is included free for the minimum 4-hour charter. There are no hidden costs – just bring your sun cream and a spirit of celebration. For pricing on other boats, contact us via WhatsApp.

Suggested Itinerary for Hispanic Day

Start your day at Puerto Banús, where you can board your yacht and enjoy a welcome drink. Head west towards Cabopino or east to Marbella Marina, then anchor at Cala Cortés or Cala del Faro for a swim in the 21°C sea. The skipper can also take you past the iconic La Concha mountain backdrop. For a longer charter, cruise to Estepona or Sotogrande. Return to Puerto Banús in the late afternoon to catch local Hispanic Day festivities along the waterfront.

Pricing Overview

All prices include skipper, drinks, snacks, fuel, insurance, and IVA. For a full list of boats, visit our Marbella boat rental page.

Book Your Hispanic Day Charter

Reserve your yacht early, as 12 October is a popular date. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book directly at the prices above. For other vessels, contact us on WhatsApp for availability and custom quotes. We recommend at least two weeks' notice to secure your preferred boat. All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's most prestigious marina. Celebrate Hispanic Day with style, comfort, and the freedom of the sea.

Frequently asked questions

What is Hispanic Day and why celebrate it in Marbella?

Hispanic Day (Día de la Hispanidad) on 12 October commemorates Spain's cultural heritage and ties with Latin America. Marbella, with its Mediterranean sunshine and luxury lifestyle, offers a refined way to honour the day – a yacht charter from Puerto Banús combines celebration with sea views, fine drinks, and the spirit of Spanish hospitality.

Which yachts are available for Hispanic Day charters?

Our fleet includes over 15 boats departing from Puerto Banús. For smaller groups, the Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) are ideal. Mid-size options like the Astondoa 40 (9 pax, from €749/2h) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) work well for families. Larger groups can book the Mangusta 80 (12 pax, from €4,719/4h) or Ferretti 94 (12 pax). All prices include skipper, drinks, and snacks.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You also get water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There are no hidden fees – just show up at Puerto Banús and enjoy. For Tier B boats (Mangusta 80), a jet ski is included free for the first 4 hours.

Can we watch the Hispanic Day parade from the yacht?

While the main national parade is in Madrid, Marbella hosts local celebrations including cultural events and tapas festivals. From your yacht, you can enjoy the Mediterranean backdrop, anchor near the coast, and listen to Spanish music on board. Many clients combine a morning charter with an afternoon stroll along the Golden Mile.

How do I book a yacht for Hispanic Day?

Booking is simple: contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on specific boats. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book directly online at the listed prices. We recommend reserving at least 2 weeks in advance for 12 October, as demand is high. All charters depart from Puerto Banús.

What is the weather like in Marbella on Hispanic Day?

October in Marbella is mild and pleasant. Average daytime temperatures range from 20°C to 25°C, with sea temperatures around 21°C – comfortable for swimming. Sunshine is common, though a light jacket is advisable for evening returns. The sea is generally calm, making for smooth cruising along the Costa del Sol.

Can we bring our own food or decorations on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food, cakes, or decorations to personalise your celebration. The boat comes with a cooler and basic snacks. For larger groups, we can arrange catering from local Marbella restaurants – just let us know in advance. Note that glass bottles are discouraged for safety.

Are there any age restrictions for children on board?

Children are welcome on all our yachts. Life jackets are provided for all ages, including infants. The skipper will brief you on safety procedures. For families, the Lagoon 380 (10 pax) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) offer spacious decks. There is no minimum age, but parents are responsible for supervision.
